content.text: Jāmi` at-Tirmidhī is a collection of hadīth compiled by Imām Abū `Isa Muhammad at-Tirmidhī (rahimahullāh). It is one of the six canonical collections of hadith (Kutub as-Sittah) and contains roughly 4,400 hadīth (with repetitions) in 46 books.

Abū `Isa Muhammad ibn `Isa ibn Sawrah ibn Musa ibn al-Ḍaḥḥāk al-Sulamī at-Tirmidhī (209–279 AH / 824–892 CE) devoted his life to Hadith, studied under Imam al-Bukhari, Imam Muslim and Imam Abu Dawud, travelled widely and presented his Jami` to the scholars of Hijaz, Iraq and Khurasan.
He was renowned for his extraordinary memory and could accurately recite lengthy collections of hadith from memory.
His Jami` combines hadith grading, narrator analysis and fiqh discussions, bringing together the methodologies of earlier compilers.
Among his famous students were Haytham ibn Kulaib, Abul Abbaas and Muhammad ibn Ahmad Shah Abdul Aziz.
He passed away in 279 AH at the age of 70 in Bawag.

items: It is both a Sunan and a Jami`.
